step_by_step_thinking:
The correct antibiotic for treating bacterial vaginosis in pregnancy would typically include metronidazole because it has good penetration through the placenta and is effective against anaerobic bacteria commonly involved in BV.
\boxed{B} Based on the information given about the effectiveness and safety profile of metronidazole in pregnancy, particularly regarding its ability to penetrate the placental barrier and treat anaerobic infections common in bacterial vaginosis, option B appears to be the best choice.
